title: Isotoma longiflora
intro: Isotoma longiflora, commonly known as blue star, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of I. longiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
